    returned no detail; callers should rely on Code, HTTPStatus, or RequestID
    for programmatic handling.

type ErrorCode string
    ErrorCode is the stable wire enum from api.isa.v1.ErrorCode. Clients switch
    on these values rather than HTTP status because the server's status mapping
    is policy that may evolve.

const (
	ErrorCodeUnspecified        ErrorCode = ""
	ErrorCodeUnauthorized       ErrorCode = "unauthorized"
	ErrorCodeTokenExpired       ErrorCode = "token_expired"
	ErrorCodeInvalidToken       ErrorCode = "invalid_token"
	ErrorCodeForbidden          ErrorCode = "forbidden"
	ErrorCodeNotFound           ErrorCode = "not_found"
	ErrorCodeMethodNotAllowed   ErrorCode = "method_not_allowed"
	ErrorCodeConflict           ErrorCode = "conflict"
	ErrorCodeValidationError    ErrorCode = "validation_error"
	ErrorCodeLicenseLocked      ErrorCode = "license_locked"
	ErrorCodeRateLimitExceeded  ErrorCode = "rate_limit_exceeded"
	ErrorCodeInternalError      ErrorCode = "internal_error"
	ErrorCodeBadGateway         ErrorCode = "bad_gateway"
	ErrorCodeGatewayTimeout     ErrorCode = "gateway_timeout"
	ErrorCodeServiceUnavailable ErrorCode = "service_unavailable"
	ErrorCodeNotImplemented     ErrorCode = "not_implemented"
)
    Known error codes. The set mirrors api.isa.v1.ErrorCode at the time of
    writing; unknown codes are surfaced as base *Error with the raw code
    preserved.

type ForbiddenError struct{ Err *Error }
    ForbiddenError wraps 403 responses.

func (e *ForbiddenError) Error() string
    Error satisfies the error interface; see UnauthorizedError.Error.

func (e *ForbiddenError) Unwrap() error
    Unwrap exposes the base *Error for errors.As traversal.

type InvalidTokenError struct{ Err *Error }
    InvalidTokenError wraps ERROR_CODE_INVALID_TOKEN.

func (e *InvalidTokenError) Error() string
    Error satisfies the error interface; see UnauthorizedError.Error.

func (e *InvalidTokenError) Unwrap() error
    Unwrap exposes the base *Error for errors.As traversal.

type NotFoundError struct{ Err *Error }
    NotFoundError wraps 404 responses.

func (e *NotFoundError) Error() string
    Error satisfies the error interface; see UnauthorizedError.Error.

func (e *NotFoundError) Unwrap() error
    Unwrap exposes the base *Error for errors.As traversal.

type NotImplementedError struct{ Err *Error }
    NotImplementedError is returned for surfaces the SDK exposes but the server
    has not yet shipped (e.g. Documents.Cancel today).

func (e *NotImplementedError) Error() string
    Error satisfies the error interface; see UnauthorizedError.Error.

func (e *NotImplementedError) Unwrap() error
    Unwrap exposes the base *Error for errors.As traversal.

type Options struct {
	// BaseURL overrides DefaultBaseURL. Trailing slash is trimmed.
	BaseURL string
	// HTTPClient overrides the default http.Client. The provided client
	// is wrapped — its existing transport is preserved, bearer and
	// retry transports are layered on top.
	HTTPClient *http.Client
	// UserAgent is appended to outbound requests as the User-Agent
	// header. Empty defaults to a versioned SDK string.
	UserAgent string
	// MaxAttempts caps total request attempts including the first.
	// Zero falls back to coretransport.DefaultMaxAttempts.
	MaxAttempts int

	// Has unexported fields.
}
    Options configures a Client at construction. All fields are optional;
    New supplies safe defaults for omitted values.

type PdfSource struct {
	URL          string `json:"url"`
	ExpectedHash string `json:"expected_hash,omitempty"`
}
    PdfSource describes one input PDF the renderer must fetch and merge into
    the packet. ExpectedHash is optional; when set the renderer aborts with a
    validation error on hash mismatch.

type RateLimitedError struct{ Err *Error }
    RateLimitedError wraps 429 responses. RetryAfter is populated from
    Retry-After when present (delta-seconds or HTTP-date); zero when absent.

func (e *RateLimitedError) Error() string
    Error satisfies the error interface; see UnauthorizedError.Error.

func (e *RateLimitedError) Unwrap() error
    Unwrap exposes the base *Error for errors.As traversal.

type Recipient struct {
	Email string `json:"email"`
	Name  string `json:"name,omitempty"`
}
    Recipient identifies who will sign the envelope and where the signer
    notification email is dispatched. Name is optional and used as the To-header
    display name.

type SendOptions struct {
	IdempotencyKey string
	SessionID      string
}
    SendOptions overrides the auto-generated request metadata. Production
    callers leave this empty; integrators that already issued an idempotency key
    for at-least-once delivery from a queue pass it here.

type SendRequest struct {
	Packet        []PdfSource       `json:"packet"`
	Recipient     Recipient         `json:"recipient"`
	LegalText     string            `json:"legal_text,omitempty"`
	TTL           string            `json:"ttl,omitempty"`
	UserAgent     string            `json:"user_agent,omitempty"`
	IsProduction  bool              `json:"is_production,omitempty"`
	RemoteAllowed *bool             `json:"remote_allowed,omitempty"`
	TemplateData  map[string]any    `json:"data,omitempty"`
	Metadata      map[string]string `json:"metadata,omitempty"`
}
    SendRequest is the input to Documents.Send.

    LegalText is embedded beneath the signature block when non-empty.
    TTL follows the ISO-8601 duration format used by the wire (`P30D`);
    an empty value applies the service default. Metadata is opaque to the client
    and stored verbatim for the audit trail.

type ServiceUnavailableError struct{ Err *Error }
    ServiceUnavailableError wraps 503 / ERROR_CODE_SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE.

func (e *ServiceUnavailableError) Error() string
    Error satisfies the error interface; see UnauthorizedError.Error.

func (e *ServiceUnavailableError) Unwrap() error
    Unwrap exposes the base *Error for errors.As traversal.

type Signature struct {
	SignID    string            `json:"sign_id"`
	Signature []byte            `json:"-"`
	SignedAt  time.Time         `json:"signed_at"`
	SignerIP  string            `json:"signer_ip,omitempty"`
	UserAgent string            `json:"user_agent,omitempty"`
	Metadata  map[string]string `json:"metadata,omitempty"`
}
    Signature is the captured signing event. Signature is the
    already-base64-decoded image bytes; SignedAt is server-stamped.

type TokenExpiredError struct{ Err *Error }
    TokenExpiredError wraps ERROR_CODE_TOKEN_EXPIRED.

func (e *TokenExpiredError) Error() string
    Error satisfies the error interface; see UnauthorizedError.Error.

func (e *TokenExpiredError) Unwrap() error
    Unwrap exposes the base *Error for errors.As traversal.

type UnauthorizedError struct{ Err *Error }
    UnauthorizedError wraps ERROR_CODE_UNAUTHORIZED / 401 responses.

func (e *UnauthorizedError) Error() string
    Error forwards to the embedded base error so the typed subclass satisfies
    the error interface (the embedded field's `Error` name shadows the method).

func (e *UnauthorizedError) Unwrap() error
    Unwrap exposes the base *Error so errors.As/errors.Is can climb to the
    underlying value when callers prefer matching on the common type.

type ValidationError struct {
	Err   *Error
	Field string
}
    ValidationError wraps 400 / ERROR_CODE_VALIDATION_ERROR responses.
    Field is set when the server identified a single offending field via the
    problem-details `param` extension.

func (e *ValidationError) Error() string
    Error satisfies the error interface; see UnauthorizedError.Error.
    When a Field is present it is appended to the detail for human readability
    (`<detail> (field=<name>)`).

func (e *ValidationError) Unwrap() error
    Unwrap exposes the base *Error for errors.As traversal.

type WebhookEvent struct {
	ID        string         `json:"id"`
	Type      string         `json:"type"`
	SignID    string         `json:"sign_id"`
	Status    DocumentStatus `json:"status"`
	CreatedAt int64          `json:"created_at"`
}
    WebhookEvent is the canonical decoded event payload. Fields are reserved for
    the upcoming webhook surface (#38); the type ships today so consumers can
    write code against it.

type Webhooks struct {
	// Has unexported fields.
}
    Webhooks groups webhook-related helpers on a Client. The Verify
    method is reserved for the upcoming HMAC signature surface and returns
    *NotImplementedError until that surface lands.

func (w *Webhooks) Verify(rawBody []byte, headers http.Header, secret string) (*WebhookEvent, error)
    Verify will check the HMAC signature on a raw webhook body, parse the JSON
    envelope, and return the typed event. The server-side signing surface is
    still being designed (#38); for now this method returns *NotImplementedError
    so SDK consumers can wire the call site against the eventual shape.


=== package proxy ===
package proxy // import "github.com/Software-Automation-Holdings-LLC/sdk/proxy"

Package proxy exposes `proxy.Call()` — structured invocation against the ISA
Platform `/v1/call` endpoint, signed with canonical session- credential HMAC.

Wire envelope (opaque pass-through; do NOT flatten):

    { integration_id | integration_uuid, method, params }

Auth headers come from core.SignRequest (the canonical session signer);
Idempotency-Key is auto-minted as a UUID v4 when the caller omits one. The
SDK↔proxy hop is HMAC-signed; the proxy↔downstream hop remains Algosure HMAC and
is handled server-side (ADR-035, amended in PR #<this>).

CONSTANTS

const DefaultProxyOrigin = "https://proxy.isaapi.com"
    DefaultProxyOrigin is the production origin for the platform proxy.


FUNCTIONS

func Call(ctx context.Context, b SessionBinding, opts CallOptions) ([]byte, error)
    Call invokes a registered integration through the platform proxy.

    Returns the raw response body bytes. Non-2xx responses are surfaced as typed
    errors:

    - 401 → *zyins.AuthError
    - 400 → *zyins.ValidationError
    - 409 with code=idempotency_conflict → *zyins.IdempotencyConflictError
    - everything else → *zyins.Error


TYPES

type CallOptions struct {
	// IntegrationUUID is the preferred opaque identifier.
	IntegrationUUID string
	// IntegrationID is the legacy BIGSERIAL identifier. Must be positive when used.
	IntegrationID int64
	// Params is the opaque payload forwarded to the downstream integration.
	Params any
	// Method overrides the downstream HTTP method (default "POST").
	Method string
	// IdempotencyKey is auto-minted as a UUID v4 when empty.
	IdempotencyKey string

	// HTTPClient overrides the default client (test seam).
	HTTPClient *http.Client
	// Now pins the signing timestamp (test seam).
	Now time.Time
	// UUIDFactory overrides the default UUID v4 generator (test seam).
	UUIDFactory func() string
}
    CallOptions parameterize one invocation. Exactly one of IntegrationUUID /
    IntegrationID must be set; both or neither returns a *zyins.ValidationError
    before any network work.

type SessionBinding struct {
	SessionID     string
	SessionSecret string //nolint:gosec // documented credential field
	ProxyOrigin   string
}
    SessionBinding carries the credentials and origin a Call needs. Built by the
    parent Isa client at construction; tests build it directly.
